automotive-ev-battery.title: US lawmakers propose annual EV fee in transport bill
automotive-ev-battery.summary: US lawmakers have introduced legislation proposing a new annual federal fee for electric vehicles and plug-in hybrids as part of a five-year transport package. The bill would simultaneously reduce funding for EV charging infrastructure and electrified transport programs, marking a significant policy shift that could impact EV adoption rates.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Bosch secures major order from Mercedes-Benz for electric motors
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Bosch has won a significant contract from Mercedes-Benz to manufacture high-volume electric motors for the next generation of electric drives through the 2030s. The order underscores continued demand from major automakers for key EV powertrain components and Bosch's strategic position as a leading automotive supplier.

automotive-ev-battery.title: SBRS introduces 500 kW charging system for commercial vehicles
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Shell subsidiary SBRS has launched the Shell Recharge PowerPack 500, a modular DC charging solution designed for heavy-duty commercial vehicles entering the European market. The system delivers up to 500 kW and is engineered for continuous operation in commercial transport applications, addressing a critical infrastructure gap for fleet electrification.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Reading to install 2,600 on-street EV charge points with char.gy
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Reading Borough Council has partnered with charge point operator char.gy to deploy approximately 2,600 public EV chargers across residential streets in a 15-year project. The initiative aims to ensure that at least 90 percent of households without private driveways have access to a charger within 100 meters, addressing accessibility challenges for urban EV adoption.

automotive-ev-battery.title: ZF commits to development and production of e-motors and inverters
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Automotive supplier ZF has completed a strategic review of its E-Division and confirmed its commitment to continued manufacturing of electric motors and inverters. The decision demonstrates confidence in the long-term demand for core EV powertrain components among major suppliers.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Einride and EASE logistics plan pilot project for autonomous trucks in Ohio
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Swedish autonomous vehicle company Einride and US freight carrier EASE Logistics have announced a pilot project deploying two driverless Einride eBots for commercial operations in Ohio. The initiative represents a significant step toward commercializing autonomous electric heavy-duty transport in the United States.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Pod Point acquires EO Charging in the UK
automotive-ev-battery.summary: EDF-owned Pod Point has acquired EO Charging, a specialist in EV fleet charging and depot charging software solutions that recently entered administration. The acquisition strengthens Pod Point's market position and service offerings in the UK commercial EV charging sector.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Xpeng launches robotaxi series production
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese automaker Xpeng has begun series production of a robotaxi based on its electric SUV platform, marking the first robotaxi to be fully developed in China and move into mass production. The milestone demonstrates China's advancing capabilities in autonomous vehicle technology and serves as a significant competitor development for global automotive markets.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Australia starts building its own electric ferries
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Brisbane-based Aus Ships Group is constructing an electric ferry in collaboration with Volvo Penta, expected to launch by year-end, while New South Wales has approved a second shipbuilder to build a 24-meter battery electric ferry. The projects represent Australia's emerging domestic capacity for electric maritime transport.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Introducing the Škoda Epiq: the beloved compact SUV
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Volkswagen Group has unveiled the Škoda Epiq as the third electric compact SUV in its new family of affordable EV models, following the ID. Polo and Cupra Raval. Starting at €25,900, the vehicle targets consumers seeking affordable electric mobility in the expanding small car segment.

finance-markets.title: EU Expedites Start of US Trade Deal in Bow to Trump Demand
finance-markets.summary: The European Union has finalized the text of its long-delayed trade agreement with the United States, clearing a major regulatory hurdle ahead of President Trump's threatened tariff deadline. The expedited completion signals EU concessions to Trump's demands and reduces the risk of transatlantic trade escalation.

finance-markets.title: Singapore Tops Indonesia as Biggest Southeast Asia Stock Market
finance-markets.summary: Singapore has surpassed Indonesia to become Southeast Asia's largest stock market, reflecting investor confidence in Singapore's market reforms and diminishing optimism regarding Indonesia's economic management. The shift represents a significant realignment of regional capital flows and investor sentiment in the world's fastest-growing region.

finance-markets.title: Trump and sons granted 'forever' immunity from existing tax audits
finance-markets.summary: The Justice Department has granted President Trump and his sons permanent immunity from current and future tax audits, a move announced one day after the government established a $1.8 billion fund to compensate the president's allies allegedly harmed by prior legal actions. The unprecedented immunity arrangement raises questions about equal treatment under tax law.

finance-markets.title: Palm Oil Stocks Drop on Indonesia Plan to Tighten Export Control
finance-markets.summary: Indonesian palm oil producer stocks declined sharply following announcements of stricter state control over commodity exports in Southeast Asia's largest economy. The tighter regulations are expected to impact profitability and capital allocation for the region's major palm oil exporters.

finance-markets.title: Kokusai Electric Shares Decline as KKR Sells All Its Stake
finance-markets.summary: Shares of Japanese chipmaking equipment supplier Kokusai Electric Corp. declined after its largest shareholder, private equity firm KKR & Co., announced it is divesting its entire stake in the company. The divestment signals a shift in KKR's portfolio strategy and may indicate changing valuations in the semiconductor equipment sector.

finance-markets.title: Global Banks Fill Void in Australian AT1 Market After Phaseout
finance-markets.summary: Following Australia's banking regulator decision to phase out Additional Tier 1 bonds issued by domestic lenders, international banks are moving to fill the market void left by the exit of local issuers. The shift redistributes riskier bank debt issuance from Australian to global financial institutions.

finance-markets.title: Morgan Stanley issues China-only iPhones to its Hong Kong bankers
finance-markets.summary: Morgan Stanley has distributed specialized iPhones restricted to use only in mainland China to its Hong Kong-based bankers, reflecting heightened concerns over data security and surveillance risks for staff traveling between the two territories. The measure underscores growing tensions around financial sector data protection in the region.

finance-markets.title: Putin and Xi to discuss huge gas project at China summit
finance-markets.summary: Russian President Putin and Chinese leader Xi Jinping are scheduled to discuss the Power of Siberia 2 pipeline project during an upcoming summit, occurring just days after President Trump's visit to Beijing. The energy infrastructure discussions highlight deepening Russia-China strategic alignment and alternative energy partnerships outside Western markets.

finance-markets.title: Google to release smart glasses and add AI 'agents' to search engine
finance-markets.summary: Google CEO Sundar Pichai announced the company will launch smart glasses and deploy AI agents powered by the new Gemini model across its search platform to narrow the competitive gap with OpenAI and Anthropic. The product expansions represent Google's major push to reassert dominance in the rapidly evolving artificial intelligence market.

finance-markets.title: US to cut troops in Europe to lowest level since before Ukraine invasion
finance-markets.summary: The Pentagon plans to reduce its military deployment in Europe from four brigades to three, bringing troop levels to their lowest point since Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine. The drawdown reflects shifting US defense priorities and potential recalibration of strategic commitments to NATO allies.

world-news.title: Ebola outbreak may be spreading faster than first thought, WHO doctor warns
world-news.summary: The World Health Organization has warned that the Ebola outbreak in central Africa may be spreading more rapidly than initially detected, with hundreds of suspected cases across the region. Health experts fear the actual number of infections significantly exceeds official counts, posing a critical global health threat.

world-news.title: 'Ebola has tortured us': Fear grips eastern DR Congo as deadly virus spreads
world-news.summary: The Democratic Republic of Congo's health minister has acknowledged that medical teams have been unable to keep pace with the rapidly spreading Ebola virus due to delays in detecting cases. The outbreak has created widespread fear across eastern regions as transmission continues.

world-news.title: Iran war live: Tehran warns of 'many more surprises' if conflict resumes
world-news.summary: Iran has issued warnings of additional military action should conflict resume, following U.S. President Trump's threats of military strikes and demands for negotiations within a two to three-day timeframe. The escalating rhetoric raises tensions in a critical geopolitical region.

world-news.title: US Senate advances resolution to curb Trump's power to wage war on Iran
world-news.summary: The U.S. Senate has advanced a War Powers Resolution to restrict the president's authority to conduct military operations against Iran, marking a rare congressional rebuke as pressure increases to prevent further military action. The vote reflects growing concern over escalating U.S.-Iran tensions.

world-news.title: Putin meets Xi: Why Russia and China need each other
world-news.summary: Vladimir Putin's visit to Beijing comes immediately after U.S. President Trump's departure from the Chinese capital, underscoring deepening Russia-China strategic alignment. The meeting highlights the mutual interests binding the two nations amid ongoing geopolitical competition with Western powers.

world-news.title: Thousands of protesters demand president's resignation in Bolivia's La Paz
world-news.summary: Bolivia is experiencing its worst economic crisis in four decades, prompting widespread street protests across La Paz demanding the resignation of the sitting president. The political unrest reflects deepening socioeconomic instability across the nation.

world-news.title: Estonia says Nato jet shot down drone over its territory
world-news.summary: Estonia has reported that a NATO jet downed a drone over Estonian airspace, which the country suspects was a Ukrainian projectile that lost trajectory due to Russian electronic jamming. The incident raises concerns about cross-border military operations and electronic warfare capabilities in the region.

world-news.title: US authorities say missionary who contracted Ebola en route to Germany
world-news.summary: A missionary has contracted Ebola while traveling toward Germany, highlighting the risk of international disease transmission during the ongoing central African outbreak. The World Health Organization reports the death toll has reached 134 cases amid continued transmission challenges.

world-news.title: Thailand cuts visa-free stay period for more than 90 countries including UK
world-news.summary: Thailand has reduced the visa-free entry period from 60 to 30 days for citizens of over 90 nations, including the United Kingdom, requiring affected visitors to obtain visas for extended stays. The policy shift will impact international travel patterns to the Southeast Asian nation.

world-news.title: Video shows oil spill on Iranian island after refinery attack last month
world-news.summary: Recent video documentation reveals the environmental damage from an oil spill caused by an attack on Iran's Lavan refinery, exposing the broader ecological consequences of ongoing regional military strikes. The incident underscores the environmental toll of Middle Eastern tensions.

science-space.title: NASA's Psyche Mission Aces Mars Flyby, Targets Metal-Rich Asteroid
science-space.summary: NASA's Psyche spacecraft successfully completed a critical gravity-assist flyby of Mars on May 15, 2026, approaching within 2,864 miles of the planet's surface. The maneuver provided essential velocity boost and orbital plane adjustment without consuming onboard propellant, redirecting the spacecraft toward its primary destination: a metal-rich asteroid.

science-space.title: NASA satellite will test orbital 'gas station' tech to help astronauts reach the moon and Mars
science-space.summary: NASA's Liquid Oxygen Flight Demonstration (LOXSAT) satellite will test critical technologies for in-space transfer and storage of cryogenic propellants, capabilities essential for the Artemis program. These advances are fundamental to enabling future human missions to the Moon and Mars.

science-space.title: Psyche's High-Resolution View of Mars' South Pole
science-space.summary: NASA's Psyche mission captured the highest-resolution imagery to date of Mars' water ice-rich south polar cap following its gravity-assist flyby. The images, with a resolution of approximately 0.7 miles per pixel, reveal unprecedented detail of the polar region extending over 430 miles.

science-space.title: Lasers in moon craters could create a lunar GPS system
science-space.summary: Scientists are developing a GPS-like navigation network using lasers positioned in the Moon's coldest, darkest craters to enable precise positioning for future Artemis astronauts and spacecraft. This technology could significantly enhance navigation accuracy on the lunar surface.

science-space.title: Johnson's Cindy Evans Prepares Artemis Teams for Lunar Science
science-space.summary: NASA's Artemis II crew served as scientific ambassadors during their historic lunar mission, conducting geological analysis and photography of the Moon's far side during their 10-day journey. The crew documented critical lunar features including impact craters and other geologic formations.

science-space.title: Scientists just solved a tricky asteroid-hopping spacecraft riddle
science-space.summary: Researchers have developed a novel mathematical approach to solve the complex optimization problem of plotting optimal trajectories for spacecraft visiting multiple asteroids. The breakthrough addresses a computationally challenging variant of the classic Traveling Salesperson problem.

science-space.title: NASA's Psyche Mission Spies Mars' Wind-Blown Craters During Close Approach
science-space.summary: During its May 15, 2026 close approach, NASA's Psyche spacecraft captured detailed imagery of wind-carved streaks extending up to 30 miles across the Syrtis Major region. The high-resolution images reveal how Martian wind patterns have shaped the surface features around impact craters.

science-space.title: NASA's New Shock Detectives Project Invites Volunteers to Help Study Solar Wind
science-space.summary: NASA's Magnetospheric Multiscale (MMS) mission has accumulated over a decade of solar wind data exceeding scientists' analysis capacity, prompting the agency to launch a citizen science initiative. Volunteers as 'Shock Detectives' will help classify chaotic and peaceful regions in magnetospheric data to advance solar wind research.

science-space.title: NASA's Psyche Mission Images Mars' Huygens Crater
science-space.summary: NASA's Psyche mission captured enhanced-color multispectral imagery of Mars' large double-ring Huygens Crater, approximately 290 miles in diameter, and the surrounding heavily cratered southern highlands. The varied colors in the imagery likely reflect compositional differences in the Martian surface.

science-space.title: Vast, builder of private space stations, launches line of high-power satellites
science-space.summary: Vast, the California-based startup developing private space stations, has announced a new commercial product line of high-power satellites. The expansion diversifies the company's portfolio beyond orbital infrastructure development.

tech-ai.title: With Gemini 3.5 Flash, Google bets its next AI wave on agents, not chatbots
tech-ai.summary: Google launched Gemini 3.5 Flash, its most powerful coding and agentic AI model to date, at its annual developer conference. The model is capable of autonomously executing complex tasks and building software from scratch, marking a strategic shift toward AI agents as the primary interface for user interaction.

tech-ai.title: How to use Google's new AI agents to go beyond your standard searches
tech-ai.summary: Google is launching AI-powered 'information agents' that monitor topics in the background and proactively alert users to updates and changes. These agents represent a fundamental shift in how users interact with search and information discovery.

tech-ai.title: Google's Genie world model can now simulate real streets with Street View
tech-ai.summary: Google DeepMind has integrated Street View with Project Genie to create immersive, interactive world simulations for robotics, gaming, and travel applications. The advancement enables users to explore real environments, simulate weather changes, and test rare scenarios in a virtual setting.

tech-ai.title: You can now talk to your Gmail inbox, as seen at Google IO 2026
tech-ai.summary: Google has expanded Gmail's AI Inbox with conversational voice search capabilities, allowing users to ask Gemini to locate and retrieve specific email details using natural language. The enhancement makes email management more accessible through voice interaction.

tech-ai.title: Google takes a page out of Meta's book, announces new audio-powered smart glasses at IO 2026
tech-ai.summary: Google unveiled new audio-powered smart glasses that enable users to issue verbal commands and perform tasks through its ecosystem of apps and services, including Gemini. The devices represent Google's entry into the competitive smart glasses market.

tech-ai.title: Google just declared itself a contender in AI design at IO 2026
tech-ai.summary: Google announced new AI-powered design tools aimed at making artificial intelligence accessible to a broad audience including teachers and small business owners. The company is positioning itself as a major player in democratizing AI design capabilities.

tech-ai.title: From teen hacker to Iron Dome researcher, this founder raised $28M to fight AI phishing
tech-ai.summary: Ocean, an agentic email security platform, has raised $28 million to combat AI-powered phishing and email fraud. The platform uses AI to analyze the contextual elements of incoming emails to detect sophisticated impersonation attempts and fraudulent communications.

tech-ai.title: Discord enables end-to-end encrypted voice and video calling for every user
tech-ai.summary: Discord has enabled end-to-end encryption for voice and video calls across its hundreds of millions of users, ensuring that communications are scrambled and unreadable even to Discord itself. The security enhancement strengthens privacy protections for the platform's user base.

tech-ai.title: Google's AI future demands trust — and your personal data
tech-ai.summary: Google presented an ambitious AI-powered future at I/O 2026 featuring new tools like Gemini Spark and Daily Brief, but many features depend on access to and analysis of users' personal data. The company's strategy raises important questions about privacy trade-offs in exchange for AI-driven convenience.

tech-ai.title: Mach Industries just spent $50M to solve a major defense tech problem
tech-ai.summary: Mach Industries completed a $50 million acquisition to address a critical challenge in defense technology production economics. The company stated that the acquisition significantly improves unit economics across its five vehicle programs at a critical scaling moment.

startups-vc.title: Venture Capital Is Concentrating Faster Than Ever. What Happens To Everyone Else?
startups-vc.summary: U.S. venture capital is concentrating at an accelerating pace, with 80% of startup investment through April 2026 directed toward rounds of $500 million and above, matching all of 2025's funding levels in just four months. This trend highlights a widening disparity in capital access across the startup ecosystem.

startups-vc.title: Marketing operating system Nectar Social raises $30M Series A led by Menlo
startups-vc.summary: Nectar Social, an AI-powered marketing platform, announced a $30 million Series A funding round led by Menlo Ventures and its Anthology Fund, which was established in partnership with Anthropic. The round underscores investor confidence in enterprise AI applications for marketing operations.

startups-vc.title: From teen hacker to Iron Dome researcher, this founder raised $28M to fight AI phishing
startups-vc.summary: Ocean, an agentic email security platform, raised $28 million to combat AI-driven phishing and impersonation attacks using advanced contextual analysis of incoming emails. The funding reflects growing enterprise demand for sophisticated AI security solutions.

startups-vc.title: $60B AI chip darling Cerebras almost died early on, burning $8M a month
startups-vc.summary: Cerebras Systems, which completed a $60 billion IPO in early 2026, faced existential financial challenges in its early years while burning through $8 million monthly to develop its revolutionary chip technology. The company's survival and subsequent success illustrates the long-term capital requirements and execution risks in advanced semiconductor development.

startups-vc.title: Stilta raises $10.5M from a16z and YC to help companies rediscover the patents they forgot they had
startups-vc.summary: Stilta, an AI platform automating intellectual property research and litigation analysis, secured $10.5 million in seed funding from Andreessen Horowitz and Y Combinator. The startup addresses the historically labor-intensive and expensive nature of patent litigation through AI-driven automation.

startups-vc.title: OpenAI co-founder Greg Brockman takes charge of product strategy
startups-vc.summary: OpenAI appointed co-founder Greg Brockman to oversee product strategy as the company reportedly plans to integrate ChatGPT with its Codex programming product. The leadership move reflects OpenAI's strategic prioritization of product development amid ongoing organizational changes.

startups-vc.title: Forget the feed: Status AI raises $17M to turn social media into interactive entertainment
startups-vc.summary: Status AI raised $17 million to develop a social media platform centered on interactive and immersive storytelling rather than traditional feed consumption. The platform targets next-generation users seeking engagement and participation over passive content viewing.

startups-vc.title: The haves and have-nots of the AI gold rush
startups-vc.summary: The article examines disparities within the AI boom, noting declining sentiment even among technology industry participants regarding the current landscape. It highlights concerns about unequal distribution of opportunities and capital in the AI-driven startup ecosystem.

startups-vc.title: 144 B2B APIs Graded. The Average Score is 71/100. The New AI Agent API Report Card Tells Us Where B2B Really Stands.
startups-vc.summary: SaaStr's AI Agent API Report Card evaluated 144 B2B APIs with an average score of 71/100, generating over 4,500 analyses and identifying 45 A-grade performers. The report provides a comprehensive assessment of API quality across the B2B software ecosystem.

startups-vc.title: The Crunchbase Tech Layoffs Tracker
startups-vc.summary: Over 127,000 workers at U.S.-based tech companies were laid off in mass job cuts during 2025, with layoffs continuing into 2026, according to Crunchbase News data. The tracker documents ongoing workforce reductions across the technology sector.

health-biotech.title: How AI helped treat a newborn's ultra rare disease. 'It was almost like a light switch.'
health-biotech.summary: Artificial intelligence successfully aided in diagnosing and treating a newborn with an ultra-rare genetic disease, demonstrating a breakthrough application of AI in precision medicine. The case illustrates how machine learning algorithms can accelerate identification of rare conditions that typically take years to diagnose.

health-biotech.title: This spine surgery usually costs $1,400. Under No Surprises Act arbitration? $34,000
health-biotech.summary: A new analysis reveals that the No Surprises Act's arbitration process is being exploited by some clinicians to justify extraordinarily inflated billing, with one spine surgery ballooning from $1,400 to $34,000. The findings underscore how protections intended to prevent surprise bills may inadvertently create new avenues for excessive healthcare costs.

health-biotech.title: 23andMe offers to connect users' DNA data with medical records
health-biotech.summary: 23andMe announced a partnership with HealthEx to allow users to link their genetic data with their medical records, potentially enabling more comprehensive health insights and clinical applications. The integration represents a significant step in connecting consumer genomics with formal healthcare systems.

health-biotech.title: Wave aims for monthly dosing with RNA editing treatment for AATD
health-biotech.summary: Wave Life Sciences reported encouraging data from its early-stage study of an RNA editing treatment for alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency, with evidence suggesting the therapy could be administered monthly rather than more frequently. The advancement may improve patient compliance and quality of life for individuals with this genetic lung disease.

health-biotech.title: Relay's PI3Kα inhibitor clears efficacy bar in Phase 2 vascular anomalies study
health-biotech.summary: Relay Therapeutics announced positive Phase 2 data for zovegalisib in rare vascular anomalies, exceeding market expectations and expanding the PI3Kα inhibitor's development pipeline beyond its original breast cancer indication. The results suggest broader therapeutic potential for the compound in rare genetic diseases.

health-biotech.title: Trump's cuts to foreign aid are undermining the Ebola response, insiders say
health-biotech.summary: Healthcare insiders report that the Trump administration's reductions in foreign aid have compromised the detection and response efforts for the ongoing Ebola outbreak. The funding constraints are said to have hampered critical surveillance and containment operations in affected regions.

health-biotech.title: Virginia governor vetoes legislation to create an advisory panel to lower the cost of prescription drugs
health-biotech.summary: Virginia's governor vetoed a significant legislative effort to establish an advisory panel aimed at reducing prescription drug costs in the state. The veto blocks what had been a closely watched approach to addressing drug pricing affordability.

health-biotech.title: Acceleron leaders and Westlake jumpstart new IPF biotech
health-biotech.summary: Oorja Bio launched with a $30 million Series A investment from Westlake Ventures, backed by experienced leadership from Acceleron and seeking to develop novel treatments for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis. The new biotech follows the success of Winrevair, an earlier therapy addressing difficult-to-treat lung disease.

health-biotech.title: Canadian medical societies lack sufficient conflict-of-interest policies, study finds
health-biotech.summary: A new analysis reveals that most Canadian medical societies have inadequate conflict-of-interest policies, with many policies being fragmented and limited in scope. The findings raise concerns about transparency and governance standards within these professional organizations.

health-biotech.title: BioMarin's rare disease therapy shows no clinical benefit in Phase 3 test
health-biotech.summary: BioMarin Pharmaceutical reported mixed results from its Phase 3 trial of an enzyme replacement therapy for ENPP1 deficiency, with the study failing to demonstrate meaningful clinical benefit despite the mechanism of action. The setback raises questions about the therapeutic approach for this rare genetic disorder.

cybersecurity.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
cybersecurity.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers have exploited known vulnerabilities in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users across more than 18,000 networks. The sophisticated campaign enabled state-backed actors to siphon credentials without deploying malicious software, representing a significant threat to enterprise authentication infrastructure.

cybersecurity.title: The New Phishing Click: How OAuth Consent Bypasses MFA
cybersecurity.summary: A phishing-as-a-service platform called EvilTokens compromised over 340 Microsoft 365 organizations across five countries within five weeks of launch by leveraging OAuth consent flows to bypass multi-factor authentication. The attack demonstrates a critical vulnerability in how modern authentication systems can be circumvented through social engineering.

cybersecurity.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
cybersecurity.summary: The U.S. Justice Department, alongside Canadian and German authorities, dismantled four major botnets—Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, and Mossad—that compromised over three million IoT devices and conducted record-breaking distributed denial-of-service attacks. The coordinated takedown disrupted infrastructure responsible for some of the most disruptive cyberattacks in recent history.

cybersecurity.title: 'Scattered Spider' Member 'Tylerb' Pleads Guilty
cybersecurity.summary: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the "Scattered Spider" cybercrime group pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft for orchestrating text-message phishing attacks in 2022. The attacks breached at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in the theft of tens of millions of dollars in cryptocurrency.

cybersecurity.title: Popular GitHub Action Tags Redirected to Imposter Commit to Steal CI/CD Credentials
cybersecurity.summary: Threat actors compromised the popular GitHub Actions workflow actions-cool/issues-helper, redirecting all repository tags to malicious commits that harvest and exfiltrate CI/CD credentials to attacker-controlled servers. The supply chain attack demonstrates the vulnerability of widely-used development tools to credential theft attacks.

cybersecurity.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
cybersecurity.summary: Microsoft released patches for 167 security vulnerabilities including a SharePoint Server zero-day and the "BlueHammer" Windows Defender flaw, while Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026 and Adobe Reader addressed an actively exploited remote code execution vulnerability. The volume of critical patches highlights ongoing threats across major software platforms.

cybersecurity.title: SEPPMail Secure E-Mail Gateway Vulnerabilities Enable RCE and Mail Traffic Access
cybersecurity.summary: Critical vulnerabilities in SEPPMail Secure E-Mail Gateway allow attackers to achieve remote code execution and read arbitrary email traffic from the virtual appliance. The flaws could enable attackers to access all mail communications or establish entry points into internal enterprise networks.

cybersecurity.title: Mini Shai-Hulud Pushes Malicious AntV npm Packages via Compromised Maintainer Account
cybersecurity.summary: Researchers identified a software supply chain attack affecting npm packages in the @antv ecosystem, including the widely-used echarts-for-react library with 1.1 million weekly downloads, perpetrated through a compromised maintainer account. The ongoing Mini Shai-Hulud campaign demonstrates continued vulnerability in open-source package repositories.

cybersecurity.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
cybersecurity.summary: Major software vendors including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla, and Oracle released near-record volumes of security patches as AI-powered vulnerability discovery tools prove effective at identifying flaws in human-written code. The trend demonstrates how artificial intelligence is accelerating the pace of security vulnerability detection and remediation.

cybersecurity.title: Compromised Nx Console 18.95.0 Targeted VS Code Developers with Credential Stealer
cybersecurity.summary: A malicious version of the Nx Console extension was published to the Microsoft Visual Studio Code Marketplace, affecting the widely-installed rwl.angular-console plugin used by over 2.2 million developers. The compromised extension deployed credential-stealing malware targeting developers across VS Code, Cursor, and JetBrains editors.

crypto-web3.title: Trump orders government, Fed to review crypto firms' access to payment rails
crypto-web3.summary: President Trump has issued an executive order directing the Federal Reserve to review how depository institutions may be granted access to payment services, a critical area where crypto firms have faced significant barriers. The directive represents a potential shift in the regulatory approach to cryptocurrency infrastructure access.

crypto-web3.title: The SEC wants to let newly public companies raise cash instantly in its biggest rule change in decades
crypto-web3.summary: The Securities and Exchange Commission is proposing its most significant overhaul of public listing rules in over two decades, aimed at reducing compliance costs and accelerating capital raising for newly public companies. The reforms would substantially ease the path for cryptocurrency firms to access traditional capital markets on Wall Street.

crypto-web3.title: Senator Elizabeth Warren accuses U.S. regulator of approving unqualified crypto banks
crypto-web3.summary: Senate Banking Committee ranking Democrat Elizabeth Warren has sent a letter to the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency questioning the eligibility of nine crypto firms that have received special bank charters. The inquiry reflects ongoing legislative skepticism about regulatory approval standards for cryptocurrency-focused financial institutions.

crypto-web3.title: Former BNY exec launches NUVA, bets tokenization will remake Wall Street
crypto-web3.summary: A former Bank of New York executive has launched NUVA with nearly $19 billion in tokenized real-world assets from Figure Technologies, targeting the integration of regulated U.S. yield products into decentralized finance platforms. The initiative signals accelerating mainstream financial institution interest in blockchain-based asset tokenization.

crypto-web3.title: Tokenized equities daily volume hits all-time high of $3.57 billion
crypto-web3.summary: Onchain equities trading volume has reached an all-time daily high of $3.57 billion, reflecting sustained growth momentum since the beginning of the year. The milestone demonstrates increasing institutional and retail adoption of blockchain-based equity trading infrastructure.

crypto-web3.title: Even a mountain of T-bills won't save Tether and Circle from a sudden liquidity crisis, expert says
crypto-web3.summary: A senior digital assets executive at a major German asset manager has argued that USDT and USDC do not qualify as true stablecoins due to underlying liquidity vulnerabilities, despite their substantial reserves in Treasury bills. The assessment raises questions about the resilience of the largest dollar-backed stablecoins in adverse market conditions.

crypto-web3.title: Ethereum leads $65 billion RWA race as blockchains compete for institutional tokenization flows
crypto-web3.summary: Ethereum maintains a leading position in the real-world asset tokenization market valued at $65 billion, though the decentralized market structure indicates the RWA landscape has not yet consolidated around a single dominant platform. The ongoing competition among blockchains suggests the institutional tokenization sector remains in early development.

crypto-web3.title: Stablecoin supply tops $300 billion but growth stalls as Tether gains at rivals' expense
crypto-web3.summary: Global stablecoin supply has surpassed $300 billion, yet overall growth has plateaued with market share consolidating around Tether while newer bank-issued and regulatory-compliant entrants have underperformed initial expectations. The trend suggests competitive pressures and regulatory compliance barriers are limiting expansion among alternative stablecoin providers.

crypto-web3.title: CFTC sues Minnesota over first explicit state ban on prediction markets
crypto-web3.summary: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission has filed a lawsuit challenging Minnesota's recent law explicitly banning prediction markets, the first state-level ban of its kind. The legal action could expose exchanges, payment providers, and other service providers to significant criminal liability if upheld.

crypto-web3.title: Zerohash pursues new funding at more than $1.5 billion valuation after Mastercard drops investment plans
crypto-web3.summary: Cryptocurrency infrastructure provider Zerohash is seeking new funding at a valuation exceeding $1.5 billion following Mastercard's withdrawal from an investment agreement, demonstrating continued institutional interest in crypto infrastructure despite recent corporate pullbacks. The funding pursuit signals sustained appetite among investors for companies supporting digital asset integration with traditional financial systems.

energy-climate.title: CCC: Investing in 'urgent' UK adaptation action 'cheaper than climate damages'
energy-climate.summary: The UK Climate Change Committee reports that proactive investment in climate adaptation measures such as flood defences and air conditioning infrastructure is more cost-effective than managing the damages from climate impacts. The analysis demonstrates that urgent adaptation spending delivers significant economic returns by preventing future climate-related losses.

energy-climate.title: Analysis: Wind and solar have saved UK from gas imports worth £1.7bn since Iran war began
energy-climate.summary: Renewable energy sources in the UK have displaced the need for £1.7 billion in gas imports since the outbreak of the Iran conflict, demonstrating the strategic economic and energy security benefits of wind and solar deployment. The analysis highlights how renewable capacity reduces exposure to geopolitical volatility in global energy markets.

energy-climate.title: Oman power company signs 2.7 GW PPA for continuous wind-solar-storage project
energy-climate.summary: Nama Power and Water Procurement has secured a power purchase agreement for a 2.7 GW integrated solar, wind, and battery storage project developed by O-Green Energy that will provide round-the-clock renewable power generation. The project represents a significant step toward Oman's goal of securing 7 GW of solar and 3 GW of battery storage by 2030.

energy-climate.title: Australia installs 400,000 home batteries in 10 months for 11.2 GWh
energy-climate.summary: Australia's residential energy storage sector is experiencing rapid growth with over 400,000 home batteries representing 11.2 GWh of capacity installed in the past decade months, reflecting strong consumer adoption of distributed energy resources. The deployment boom signals accelerating decentralization of Australia's electricity system and increased household energy independence.

energy-climate.title: AmpereHour and IndiGrid start up 180MW BESS in Gujarat
energy-climate.summary: AmpereHour Energy and IndiGrid have commenced operations of a 180 MW / 360 MWh battery energy storage system in Gujarat, India, expanding grid-scale storage capacity in one of Asia's largest energy markets. The project supports India's renewable energy integration and grid stability objectives.

energy-climate.title: Pakistan's operational PV capacity estimated at 51 GW
energy-climate.summary: Pakistan's solar capacity has reached approximately 51 GW of operational installations as households, farms, and businesses increasingly adopt distributed solar systems to reduce grid dependence. The distributed deployment model reflects growing economic viability and energy access improvements across Pakistan's energy sector.

energy-climate.title: Ørsted and PGE start offshore works on Baltica 2 wind farm
energy-climate.summary: Ørsted and Polish utility PGE have commenced offshore construction on the Baltica 2 wind farm in the Polish Baltic Sea sector, advancing Central European renewable energy infrastructure. The project represents significant progress in expanding offshore wind capacity in Northern Europe.

energy-climate.title: Carbon scraps solar module gigafactory project in France
energy-climate.summary: Carbon has abandoned its planned 5 GW integrated solar module manufacturing facility in Fos-sur-Mer, France, citing insufficient regulatory clarity and investor guarantees on EU industrial policy. The project cancellation reflects ongoing uncertainty affecting solar manufacturing investment in Europe despite scaling efforts and negotiations with Chinese manufacturer Longi.

energy-climate.title: EU solar recycling only viable under strict policy, say researchers
energy-climate.summary: Researchers at TU Wien conclude that binding EU policy targets are necessary to make solar module recycling economically viable, as exporting recycled materials to third countries remains the cost-optimal strategy absent regulatory mandates. The analysis underscores the need for targeted EU policy intervention to establish a circular economy for solar technology.

energy-climate.title: How opening the UK's grid to competition could cut bills and boost growth
energy-climate.summary: Analysis finds that opening the UK's electricity transmission and distribution network to greater competition could reduce consumer energy costs and stimulate economic growth. The commentary highlights how the current network infrastructure has not scaled adequately to meet contemporary demand from electrification and renewable integration.

asia-pacific.title: Japan plans island drone deployment to monitor Chinese naval activity
asia-pacific.summary:
Japan’s planned deployment of long-range surveillance drones on remote Pacific islands would mark a significant expansion of its ability to monitor Chinese naval activity, as Beijing’s warships operate more frequently beyond the first island chain.
The drones are to be stationed on Iwo Jima and Chichijima Island in the Ogasawara chain south of Tokyo, according to a report by the Yomiuri newspaper published on Monday. The airfield on Minamitorishima, about 1,950km (1,211 miles) southeast of Tokyo

asia-pacific.title: China’s rise will turn Thucydides Trap on its head
asia-pacific.summary:
The end of the Cold War was supposed to deliver a long peace. For a while, that was working, until it wasn’t. We might now be entering another period when the breakdown of a world order leads to war.
It was no accident that President Xi Jinping cited the “Thucydides Trap” during his summit in Beijing with his US counterpart Donald Trump, putting it forward as something for both superpowers to avoid. The theory, popularised by Harvard political scientist Graham Allison, digs deep into an episode.

asia-pacific.title: Japan zoo may ban photos near viral monkey Punch after intrusion by 2 Americans
asia-pacific.summary:
The zoo housing the viral Japanese macaque Punch said on Tuesday it is considering a complete ban on photography and filming around the monkey enclosure after two men were recently arrested over an intrusion incident.
The Ichikawa City Zoo in Chiba prefecture has already expanded the buffer zone around the enclosure and installed anti-intrusion netting following Sunday’s incident involving two men claiming to be US nationals.
The monkey went viral, attracting international attention, for...

asia-pacific.title: Red carpet for Putin: why China-Russia ties outpace Beijing’s engagement with Washington
asia-pacific.summary:
China has rolled out the red carpet for Russian President Vladimir Putin for his 25th visit to the country, underscoring a frequency of head-of-state diplomacy that far outpaces Beijing’s engagement with Washington.
Putin arrived in Beijing on Tuesday night for a two-day state visit, less than a week after US President Donald Trump wrapped up his own closely watched trip to China.
This marks the first time China has hosted the leaders of Russia and the US in the same month outside a multilateral

asia-pacific.title: Chinese man who lost a leg and an arm inspires many by competing in bodybuilding
asia-pacific.summary:
A man in China with only one arm and one leg who took part in a bodybuilding competition, attracting thunderous applause at site and massive admiration on social media.
The muscular Liu Xinju, 32, joined The 20th Bodybuilding Competition in Jinan, the capital city of eastern Shandong province, which was held on May 10.
Videos of his performance on stage went viral on mainland social media. Many people were moved and inspired by Liu, who consequently earned the nickname Encouraging Brother, the..

asia-pacific.title: Trump critic Massie defeated in record-breaking US primary
asia-pacific.summary:
US President Donald Trump scored another victory in his campaign to punish disloyal Republicans on Tuesday as congressman Thomas Massie of Kentucky lost his primary race, underscoring ‌the risks for lawmakers who defy Trump.
Massie, who angered Trump by leading a push to release Justice Department files tied to the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, was defeated by Ed Gallrein, a former US Navy Seal backed by the president and bolstered by heavy outside spending by pro-Israel groups, according..

asia-pacific.title: Kazakhstan to deepen financial services cooperation with Hong Kong, consul says
asia-pacific.summary:
Kazakhstan aims to deepen its financial services cooperation with Hong Kong, viewing it as an ideal fundraising hub to access renminbi capital through bond issuances, according to the country’s top envoy in the city.
In an exclusive interview with the South China Morning Post, Bauyrzhan Dosmanbetov, Kazakhstan’s consul general in Hong Kong, also said the Central Asian country’s community in the city had grown over the past decade to about 1,000, driven by the local government’s Belt and Road...

asia-pacific.title: Aluminium supply crunch threatens Asia’s clean energy push amid Iran war
asia-pacific.summary:
Asia’s clean energy push is facing heightened supply-chain risk as the Middle East conflict disrupts aluminium and nickel production and could raise costs for solar panels, wind turbines and grid upgrades.
Countries such as Indonesia, Vietnam and the Philippines have installed more rooftop solar panels and other green infrastructure in recent years, driven by high power bills caused by elevated oil and gas prices. However, these green projects are facing uncertainties as the blockade of the...

asia-pacific.title: Israeli forces fired shots at Gaza flotilla, all boats intercepted, group says
asia-pacific.summary:
Israeli forces opened fire on at least two vessels in an aid flotilla sailing towards Gaza on Tuesday, according to video footage and flotilla organisers, but Israel said no live ammunition was used ‌and there were no casualties.
The flotilla was making a renewed attempt to deliver aid to Gaza after earlier missions were intercepted by Israel in international waters.
Video from the flotilla’s live stream showed soldiers firing shots at two of the boats. The type of ammunition fired was not...

asia-pacific.title: Vance says Iran talks making ‘good progress’, but US forces remain ‘locked and loaded’
asia-pacific.summary:
US Vice-President J.D. Vance said on Tuesday that diplomatic talks between Washington and Tehran had made “good progress”, but warned that the US military was “locked and loaded” to restart its campaign against Iran if negotiations collapsed.
“A lot of good progress is being made, but we’re just going to keep on working at it, and eventually we’ll either hit a deal or we won’t,” Vance said during a White House press briefing.
“We are not going to have a deal that allows the Iranians to have a...
